'''Sprax''' was a [[Nalroni/Legends|Nalroni]], a [[Vigo/Legends|Vigo]] of Black Sun, and operator of various illegal trade groups.

==Biography==
Sprax' networks of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], pirates, and black market traders, stretched across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Sprax made a considerable fortune in trading weapons, medical supplies, and spare parts, often under Prince [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor's]] orders, favoring the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] over the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] when possible. Sprax's center of operations was located along the [[Sisar Run/Legends|Sisar Run]] near [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].

Sprax began his career as an executive within the Celanon-based [[Jatayus Trading Consortium]], but left the organization before rising to its upper-leadership, choosing instead to take a secret position in Black Sun. Under the aegis of the cartel, he served as an apprentice to a [[Unidentified Brentaal merchant house guildmaster|guildmaster]] on [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]], and later became a division supervisor with [[Xizor Transport Systems/Legends|Xizor Transport Systems]]. From XTS, he retired, wealthy and still youthful. It was then that he gained the rank of Vigo.

By c.[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], he'd grown out of his species' short-lived youth, retaining the rank despite its notoriously low survival rate. In that time, he became one of the foremost smuggler patrons and fences in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. In addition to his Black Sun rival, Vigo [[Durga Besadii Tai|Durga]], Sprax' main competition along the Sisar Run consisted of several [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] Clans and the [[Antar Band]], the latter led by [[Ujin Voli]].

==Characteristics==
By 3 ABY, Sprax was an aging male by Nalroni standards, so he concealed his graying fur with dye to give it the golden-brown hue of his youth. He wore the long tunic and tailed jackets typical of Brentaal merchants.
